**UI-for-ytdlp v0.2.0** is the first release of the redesigned application on the `main` branch. It replaces the legacy **shell-for-ytdlp** (last release: v0.1.7) with a full GUI rewrite, new architecture, and updated distribution model.

#### From shell-for-ytdlp to UI-for-ytdlp
- **New name and binary:** download `UI-for-ytdlp.exe` instead of `shell-for-ytdlp.exe`; the project identity, README, and CI now reflect the GUI-first scope
- **Complete UI rewrite:** CustomTkinter dark theme with a design system (accent `#00A8E8`, 14px rounded controls, consistent spacing scale) instead of the minimal legacy Tkinter shell
- **Modular codebase:** split into `core/` (downloader, config, tray, clipboard, sounds, updater) and `ui/` (main window, reusable components, settings dialog, tooltips)
- **Unicode icons** in the interface instead of bundled PNG toolbar assets

#### Interface and workflow
- **Main window:** URL field, live log viewer, progress bar, output path picker, and a structured settings dialog
- **System tray:** dedicated **Minimize** hides the window to the notification area; **Close (X)** fully exits and removes the tray icon; left-click / “Show window” restores; right-click menu for clipboard capture, sounds, paste-and-download, settings, and exit
- **Clipboard automation:** optional auto-download when a supported video URL is copied (~2 s polling on the Tk main thread, robust `extract_video_url` parsing, retry on read errors, duplicate-URL protection)
- **Sound feedback:** start/end download cues via pygame (toggle in settings or tray)
- **Window memory:** position and size of the main window and settings dialog persist across sessions, including DPI/scaling-aware placement

#### Downloads and utilities
- **yt-dlp** and **ffmpeg** are **not bundled in the repository**; they are downloaded automatically on first run into `./utilities/` (same user-facing idea as before, cleaner repo and CI)
- **cookies.txt** support unchanged in spirit: drop the file into `utilities/` for authenticated downloads
- SponsorBlock, playlist downloads, resume, and format selection remain core yt-dlp capabilities exposed through the new UI

#### Reliability and quality (since the legacy shell)
- **Tray lifecycle refactor:** single pystray instance (`prepare` → show/hide → safe `stop` on shutdown) fixes flaky minimize and ghost tray icons
- **Clipboard hardening:** no background-thread Tk access; immediate check on resume; handles URLs embedded in text and odd clipboard encodings
- **Encoding fixes** for yt-dlp console output on Windows
- **151 unit tests** in CI (`pytest`, integration tests optional when `yt-dlp.exe` is present)
- **GitHub Actions** build on push/PR/tag; tagged releases include VirusTotal scans and attach both `.exe` and `.zip`

#### Migration notes for existing users
- Your old `shell-for-ytdlp.exe` and `UI-for-ytdlp.exe` are separate builds — config in `utilities/config.json` is compatible in layout, but expect new UI labels and tray behavior (**Minimize** vs **Close**)
- Re-use your existing `utilities/` folder (yt-dlp, ffmpeg, cookies) where possible; the app will refresh utilities as needed
- Direct download URL pattern: `…/releases/latest/download/UI-for-ytdlp.exe`
